title: Justicia pleurolarynx
taxonomy: Justicia pleurolarynx is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. This plant is native to Central America, specifically in countries such as Costa Rica and Panama. It belongs to the genus Justicia, which is a large and diverse group of plants with over 500 species. The Acanthaceae family, to which Justicia pleurolarynx belongs, consists of over 2,000 species of flowering plants. These plants are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world.

intro: Justicia pleurolarynx, also known as Mexican Shield Plant, is a perennial herb belonging to the Acanthaceae family, native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Justicia pleurolarynx include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Justicia pleurolarynx is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, and it is also used as a topical application or added to traditional remedies such as teas, syrups, and ointments.
